subject: [HOLMES] Migrate Holmes from Cloudify to Helm
commit_message:
[HOLMES] Migrate Holmes from Cloudify to Helm

Instead of using cloudify, use helm directly in order to deploy the
charts.

Change-Id: I6c97862898ab34b63a15b942b1396c0d19179e46
Issue-ID: HOLMES-396
Signed-off-by: GuangrongFu <fu.guangrong@zte.com.cn>
[Adding AAF part and change nodeports]
Signed-off-by: Sylvain Desbureaux <sylvain.desbureaux@orange.com>

I believe you have an indentation issue somewhere.
As you're mixing several yaml ways of doing, I propose that you stick to the "kubernetes" way for list (no more space for list).

Here's what you do:

spec:
  containers:
    - name: myContainer

it should be:

spec:
  containers:
  - name: myContainer

(of course for all list in your template files).
and then verify that make + helm template works



mostly cosmetic issue + yaml anchor not well set.

Last thing you must do is to add "holmes" into AAF (see what I did on https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/117837)

you can basically do this in two ways:
- preferred: create entries for holmes-engine-mgmt and create entries for holmes-rule-mgmt. it's a bit longer but AAF checks that service name exists and thus that would simplify it.
- create entries for holmes-engine-mgmt and add holmes-rule-mgmt "SANs" ("holmes-rule-mgmt", "holmes-rule-mgmt.onap", "holmes-rule-mgmt.api.simpledemo.onap.org") into the list
